# 🎨 sense-engine.css — Technical Documentation

**SenseEngine** styles system. It defines the visual appearance, animation, and behavior of each onomatopoeia generated by the engine. It is split into four independent layers that combine with one another: **base structure, animations, natures, and skins**.

---

## 🧱 General Architecture

When the engine generates an onomatopoeia, it creates a `<div>` with stacked classes:

```html
<div class="sense-ono  [nature]  [skin]  [custom-class]">BOOM!</div>
```

> [!NOTE]
> The CSS is designed so that these layers do not interfere with each other: the nature defines color and animation, the skin modifies the visual texture on top of it, and the custom class can add any additional overrides. `.sense-ono` is always the base container for all of them.

---

## 🏗️ Layer 1 — Base Structure (`.sense-ono`)

```css
.sense-ono {
    position: absolute;
    pointer-events: none;
    user-select: none;
    font-weight: 900;
    font-size: 2rem;
    z-index: 1000;
    white-space: nowrap;
    display: inline-block;
}
```

**Every property serves a specific purpose:**

- `position: absolute` — positioned relative to the engine's container, not the document. JS handles accurate positioning using `getBoundingClientRect()`.
- `pointer-events: none` — purely decorative, preventing hit-box interference with interactive game elements.
- `user-select: none` — blocks manual mouse text highlighting.
- `font-weight: 900` — sets the maximum bold weight, ensuring the comic book impact feel.
- `font-size: 2rem` — the universal default size, which can be easily overridden.
- `z-index: 1000` — paints the string strictly above all underlying UI layers.
- `white-space: nowrap` — prevents long scripts (e.g. Arabic, Hindi) from breaking into lines and destroying animations.
- `display: inline-block` — strictly necessary for `transform` directives (scale, rotate) to work on text.

---

## 🎬 Layer 2 — Base Animations

The file declares three reusable core animations. All 'natures' consume these; there are no embedded animations inside individual nature classes except for `glitch-anim`.

### 💥 `pop`
The standard animation for conceptual impact events.
```text
0%   → scale(0)   rotate(-10deg)  opacity 0
50%  → scale(1.4) rotate(5deg)    opacity 1
100% → scale(1)   rotate(0deg)    opacity 0
```

### 💨 `slideUp`
Used for ethereal or UI feedback events.
```text
0%   → translateY(0)    opacity 1
100% → translateY(-60px) opacity 0
```

### ⚡ `flash`
Exclusive to `ono-bolt`. Infinite high-speed opacity flickering.

### 🌐 `glitch-anim`
Exclusive to `skin-glitch`. Cyclic rapid ±2px offset translations.

---

## 🌪️ Layer 3 — Natures (`ono-*`)

Natures define the **visual meaning of the event**: color, size, and animation. There are 22 bundled classes.

| Class | Base Color | Animation | Duration | Typical Usage |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| `ono-impact` | White / Red shadow | pop | 0.6s | Generic hit/strike |
| `ono-fire` | Orange | pop | 0.8s | Fire, burns |
| `ono-ice` | Cyan | pop | 1.0s | Ice, freezing |
| `ono-bolt` | Yellow | flash | infinite | Electricity, zap |
| `ono-ui` | Light Green | slideUp | 0.8s | UI interface actions |
| `ono-toxic` | Acid Green | pop | 1.0s | Poison, acid |
| `ono-heal` | White / Yellow aura | slideUp | 1.2s | Healing / Restoring |
| `ono-cyber` | Cyan monospace | pop | 0.5s | Digital / Sci-fi |

> [!TIP]
> **Extending properties:** You can manually override `font-size`, `font-family`, or force `italic` to dynamically skew any nature (check `index.html` to experiment with this).

---

## 🎭 Layer 4 — Skins (`skin-*`)

Skins modify the **visual aesthetic without touching the base color or physics/animation**. They neatly stack on top. There are 10 bundled skins:

- `skin-cartoon`: Thick 2px black stroke and drop-shadow. Pure comic book style.
- `skin-neon`: Electric glowing effect dynamically reading the inherited base color.
- `skin-glitch`: Cybernetic noise, frames overwrite simulation.
- `skin-metal`: Metallic linear gradient powered by `background-clip`.
- `skin-pixel`: Negative kerning compression mimicking an *8-bit* aesthetic.
- `skin-hollow`: Trashes the internal fill entirely, displaying solely the interactive outline stroke.
- `skin-sticker`: Die-cut sticker effect handled elegantly via `paint-order: stroke fill`.

> [!CAUTION]
> **Watch out for incompatible overrides.** Ex: `skin-metal`'s gradient cancels out the flat color of `ono-magic`.

---

## 🧩 How to expand the CSS system

### Adding a new Nature
```css
.ono-poison {
    color: #9b00ff;
    text-shadow: 0 0 12px #4b0082;
    font-style: italic;
    animation: slideUp 1.5s forwards;
}
```

### Adding `prefers-reduced-motion` support
The system includes built-in accessibility compliance for OS wide motion reduction. Ensure any new keyframes or heavy translations are covered by the final `@media` rule block in the main file:

```css
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
    .sense-ono,
    .sense-ono * {
        animation: sense-fade 0.4s forwards !important;
        transform: none !important;
    }
}
```
